executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of the research, summarizing the recommended approach for implementing window-specific video recording for Safari Pilot using ScreenCaptureKit and AVAssetWriter, and highlighting the key challenges such as permissions and CI/CD integration.

primary_capture_framework.description: The main macOS framework recommended for screen capture (e.g., ScreenCaptureKit).

encoding_library.description: The library recommended for encoding video frames (e.g., AVAssetWriter).

hardware_acceleration_api.description: The API recommended for leveraging hardware-accelerated encoding (e.g., VideoToolbox).

recommended_codec.description: The recommended video codec, noting trade-offs (e.g., H.264 for compatibility, HEVC for size).

recommended_container.description: The recommended video container format for portability (e.g., MP4).

window_discovery.description: Details on using SCShareableContent to discover and select the target Safari window.

content_filtering.description: Explanation of how to use SCContentFilter to restrict capture to a single window.

stream_configuration.description: Overview of SCStreamConfiguration options for setting resolution, frame rate, and pixel format.

frame_delivery.description: How the SCStreamOutput protocol is used to receive CMSampleBuffers containing captured frames.

direct_recording_api.description: Information on the SCRecordingOutput API (macOS 15+) for simplified, direct-to-file recording.

writer_setup.description: Guidance on setting up AVAssetWriter, AVAssetWriterInput, and AVAssetWriterInputPixelBufferAdaptor to process frames.

hardware_acceleration.description: Best practices for using VideoToolbox to enable hardware-accelerated H.264/HEVC encoding.

codec_tradeoffs.description: A summary of the trade-offs between H.264 (compatibility) and HEVC (file size) for test recordings.

real_time_encoding.description: Techniques for managing real-time encoding, including setting 'expectsMediaDataInRealTime' and handling back-pressure.

recording_mechanism.description: The underlying mechanism Playwright uses, such as the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) screencast.

output_formats.description: Default output formats and codecs used by Playwright (e.g., WebM with VP8/VP9).

lifecycle_management.description: How Playwright manages the recording lifecycle, typically on a per-test basis.

key_design_takeaways.description: Actionable lessons for Safari Pilot, such as per-test artifact isolation, metadata embedding, and robust failure handling.

obscured_windows.description: Solution for capturing window content even when it is partially or fully obscured by other windows.

window_resizing.description: Strategy for handling window resizes during recording, such as recreating or reconfiguring the SCStream.

multiple_window_identification.description: Techniques for disambiguating and selecting the correct Safari window when multiple are open.

cropping_window_chrome.description: Method for excluding the window's title bar and toolbar from the capture using SCStreamConfiguration's contentRect.

fullscreen_transitions.description: How to handle stream reconfiguration when a window enters or exits fullscreen mode.

maintenance_risk.description: The long-term maintenance risk, especially concerning API deprecation.

tcc_framework_overview.description: Explanation of macOS's Transparency, Consent, and Control (TCC) framework for Screen Recording.

programmatic_permission_apis.description: How to use CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess and CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess to check and request permission.

application_requirements.description: The requirement for the recording process to be a signed, GUI application running in an active user session.

sandboxing_and_entitlements.description: Clarification that Screen Recording is a privacy permission independent of the App Sandbox and requires no special entitlements.

hosted_runner_limitations.description: Explanation of why hosted CI runners (like GitHub Actions) cannot grant Screen Recording permission due to the lack of a GUI session.

recommended_ci_setup.description: Recommended workarounds, such as using self-hosted macOS runners with an auto-login user session.

mdm_pre_approval.description: Guidance on using Mobile Device Management (MDM) and PPPC profiles to pre-approve screen recording for managed machines.

failure_diagnostics.description: Strategies for detecting permission failures early and providing clear, actionable error messages to the user.
